Items.Title: 破解面试：你需要知道的基本高级 JavaScript 概念 | Cracking the Interview: Essential Advanced JavaScript Concepts You Need to Know
Items.Description:
无论您是初学者还是经验丰富的开发人员，都有许多高级 JavaScript 概念可以让您的技能更上一层楼。 这些概念可以帮助您编写更高效和可维护的代码，以及提高应用程序的性能和功能。 在本文中，我们将探讨一些最重要的高级 JavaScript 概念，您应该了解这些概念才能打动面试官并将您的技能提升到一个新的水平。

他们说学习高级 JavaScript 就像解锁一种超能力——除了飞行或 X 射线视觉之外，您可以制作比竞争对手的网站更酷的网站。 说实话，当您可以让您的网页元素像没人注视一样四处乱舞时，谁还需要飞呢？ 因此，准备好升级您的编码游戏并成为互联网的超级英雄 - 无需斗篷！

我们将介绍一些高级 JavaScript 概念，例如网络工作者、代理、Reflect API、符号以及迭代器和生成器。 这些概念中的每一个都有其独特的优势和用例，掌握它们可以帮助您编写更高效、灵活和可维护的代码。

例如，网络工作者允许您在单独的线程中执行代码，这可以提高您的网络应用程序的性能。 代理允许您拦截和修改对象属性的行为，使您能够实现对象隐私等高级功能。 符号允许您创建唯一的、不可变的值，这些值可以用作对象键来实现对象隐私。 迭代器和生成器使您可以更好地控制对象的迭代方式，以及生成无限值序列的能力。

使用网络工作者在单独的线程中执行昂贵或长时间运行的任务，让主线程自由处理用户输入和更新。 这可以提高 Web 应用程序的整体响应能力和性能。 例如，您可以使用网络工作者来执行复杂的计算或运行数据密集型算法而不阻塞用户界面。

用例：需要实时运行复杂物理计算或人工智能算法的基于 Web 的游戏可以使用 Web Worker 在单独的线程中处理这些进程，从而释放主线程来处理用户输入和渲染。

示例代码：

 // Create a new web worker const worker = new Worker('worker.js'); // Send a message to the worker worker.postMessage('Hello from main thread!'); // Receive a message from the worker worker.onmessage = (event) => { console.log('Message received from worker:', event.data); }; // worker.js self.onmessage = (event) => { console.log('Message received from main thread:', event.data); self.postMessage('Hello from worker!'); };

此示例说明如何使用 Worker API 创建 Web Worker 并与之通信。

代理是 JavaScript 的一项功能，可让您拦截和修改对象和函数的行为。 代理可用于实现访问控制、数据验证和动态代码生成等高级功能。

例如，您可以使用代理来实现对象隐私，确保无法从对象外部访问或修改某些属性或方法。

用例：需要提供安全可靠的方式来管理用户数据的 JavaScript 库或框架可以使用代理对数据对象的某些属性或方法实施严格的访问控制，确保敏感信息受到保护。

示例代码：

 // Create a proxy object const handler = { get(target, property) { console.log(`Getting property "${property}"`); return target[property]; }, set(target, property, value) { console.log(`Setting property "${property}" to "${value}"`); target[property] = value; return true; }, }; const obj = new Proxy({}, handler); // Use the proxy object obj.name = 'Ritik'; console.log(obj.name);

此示例说明如何使用 Proxy 构造函数创建代理对象，以及如何使用 get 和 set 陷阱拦截和修改对象属性的行为。

这可以帮助您编写更清晰、更易于维护的代码。 例如，您可以使用 Reflect API 来定义带有描述符的对象属性、访问和设置对象属性以及调用对象方法。

用例：需要显示来自第三方 API 的产品数据的电子商务网站可以使用 Reflect API 以一致且灵活的方式定义和操作产品对象的属性和方法，从而更轻松地进行集成和定制.

示例代码：

 // Create an object const obj = { name: 'Ritik' }; // Define a property using the Reflect API Reflect.defineProperty(obj, 'age', { value: 23, writable: false, }); // Get the value of a property using the Reflect API const age = Reflect.get(obj, 'age'); console.log(age); // Invoke a method using the Reflect API Reflect.apply(console.log, console, ['Hello, world!']);

此示例说明如何使用 Reflect API 以更灵活和一致的方式定义对象属性、访问对象属性和调用对象方法。

符号是 JavaScript 的一项功能，它允许您创建唯一的、不可变的值，这些值可以用作对象键或方法名称。 符号可用于实现对象隐私、方法自定义和界面设计等高级功能。

这可以帮助您实现对象隐私，因为外部代码无法访问或修改符号。 例如，您可以使用符号来定义只能从对象内部访问的私有方法或属性。

用例：需要安全存储用户凭据的密码管理器应用程序可以使用符号来定义密码对象中的私有方法和属性，以防止未经授权的访问或修改。

示例代码：

 // Define a symbol const KEY = Symbol('my secret key'); // Create an object with a private property const obj = { [KEY]: 'secret value', publicProperty: 'public value', }; // Access the private property using the symbol console.log(obj[KEY]); console.log(obj.publicProperty);

这个例子展示了如何使用符号来创建唯一的、不可变的值，这些值可以用作对象键来实现对象隐私。

迭代器和生成器是 JavaScript 的强大功能，可让您以更灵活和高效的方式迭代值集合。 迭代器和生成器可用于实现惰性求值、无限序列和自定义数据结构等高级功能。

使用迭代器和生成器来控制对象的迭代并生成值序列。 这可以帮助您编写更高效、更具表现力的代码，并启用惰性求值和无限序列等高级功能。 例如，您可以使用迭代器和生成器来实现延迟加载数据或生成无限的随机数序列。

用例：需要处理大型或复杂数据集的数据可视化工具可以使用迭代器和生成器来实现数据的延迟加载，只允许在任何给定时间加载和显示必要的数据，从而提高性能并减少内存使用。

示例代码：

 // Define an iterable object const obj = { data: [1, 2, 3, 4], [Symbol.iterator]() { let index = 0; return { next: () => { if (index < this.data.length) { return { value: this.data[index++], done: false }; } else { return { done: true }; } }, }; }, }; // Use the iterator to iterate over the object for (let value of obj) { console.log(value); } // Define a generator function function* fibonacci() { let a = 0, b = 1; while (true) { yield b; [a, b] = [b, a + b]; } } // Use the generator to generate an infinite sequence const sequence = fibonacci(); console.log(sequence.next().value); console.log(sequence.next().value); console.log(sequence.next().value);

此示例说明如何使用迭代器为对象实现自定义迭代行为，以及如何使用生成器生成无限值序列。

总之，扎实掌握这些高级 JavaScript 概念可以帮助您在拥挤的就业市场中脱颖而出，并以您的技能和知识给面试官留下深刻印象。 通过了解网络工作者、代理、Reflect API、符号、迭代器和生成器的好处和用例，您可以编写更高效、灵活和可维护的代码来满足您的用户和利益相关者的需求。 无论您是前端开发人员、后端开发人员还是全栈开发人员，这些概念对于将您的技能提升到一个新的水平并在您的职业生涯中取得成功至关重要。



原文:

JavaScript is an incredibly powerful and popular programming language that is used in a wide range of applications. Whether you are a beginner or an experienced developer, there are many advanced JavaScript concepts that can take your skills to the next level. These concepts can help you write more efficient and maintainable code, as well as improve the performance and functionality of your applications. In this article, we'll explore some of the most important advanced JavaScript concepts that you should know to impress your interviewer and take your skills to the next level.

They say that learning advanced JavaScript is like unlocking a superpower - except instead of flying or x-ray vision, you can make websites that are way cooler than your competitors'. And let's be real, who needs to fly when you can make your web page elements dance around like nobody's watching? So get ready to level up your coding game and become the superhero of the internet - no cape required!

We'll cover some advanced JavaScript concepts such as web workers, proxies, the Reflect API, symbols, and iterators and generators. Each of these concepts has its own unique benefits and use cases, and mastering them can help you write more efficient, flexible, and maintainable code.

For example, web workers allow you to execute code in a separate thread, which can improve the performance of your web applications. Proxies let you intercept and modify the behavior of object properties, enabling you to implement advanced features like object privacy. The Reflect API provides a more flexible and consistent way to define object properties, access object properties, and invoke object methods. Symbols allow you to create unique, immutable values that can be used as object keys to implement object privacy. And iterators and generators give you more control over how your objects are iterated, as well as the ability to generate infinite sequences of values.

1. Web Workers

Web Workers are a powerful feature of JavaScript that allow you to run scripts in the background, without blocking the main thread. Web Workers can help you build more responsive web applications by offloading complex and time-consuming tasks to separate threads.

Use web workers to execute expensive or long-running tasks in a separate thread, leaving the main thread free to handle user input and updates. This can improve the overall responsiveness and performance of your web application. For example, you could use web workers to perform complex calculations or run data-intensive algorithms without blocking the user interface.

Use Case: A web-based game that requires complex physics calculations or artificial intelligence algorithms to be run in real-time could use web workers to handle those processes in a separate thread, freeing up the main thread to handle user input and rendering.

Example Code:

// Create a new web worker
const worker = new Worker('worker.js'); // Send a message to the worker
worker.postMessage('Hello from main thread!'); // Receive a message from the worker
worker.onmessage = (event) => { console.log('Message received from worker:', event.data);
}; // worker.js
self.onmessage = (event) => { console.log('Message received from main thread:', event.data); self.postMessage('Hello from worker!');
};

This example shows how to create and communicate with a web worker using the Worker API.

2. Proxies

Proxies are a feature of JavaScript that allow you to intercept and modify the behavior of objects and functions. Proxies can be used to implement advanced features like access control, data validation, and dynamic code generation.

For example, you could use proxies to implement object privacy, ensuring that certain properties or methods cannot be accessed or modified from outside the object.

Use Case: A JavaScript library or framework that needs to provide a secure and reliable way to manage user data could use proxies to enforce strict access controls on certain properties or methods of the data objects, ensuring that sensitive information is protected.

Example Code:

// Create a proxy object
const handler = { get(target, property) { console.log(`Getting property "${property}"`); return target[property]; }, set(target, property, value) { console.log(`Setting property "${property}" to "${value}"`); target[property] = value; return true; },
}; const obj = new Proxy({}, handler); // Use the proxy object
obj.name = 'Ritik';
console.log(obj.name);

This example shows how to create a proxy object using the Proxy constructor, and how to intercept and modify the behavior of object properties using the get and set traps.

3. Reflect API

The Reflect API is a powerful set of methods that allow you to interact with JavaScript objects in a more flexible and consistent way. The Reflect API includes methods for creating and manipulating objects, defining and accessing object properties, and invoking object methods.

This can help you write cleaner and more maintainable code. For example, you could use the Reflect API to define object properties with descriptors, access and set object properties, and invoke object methods.

Use Case: An e-commerce website that needs to display product data from a third-party API could use the Reflect API to define and manipulate the properties and methods of the product objects in a consistent and flexible way, allowing for easier integration and customization.

Example Code:

// Create an object
const obj = { name: 'Ritik' }; // Define a property using the Reflect API
Reflect.defineProperty(obj, 'age', { value: 23, writable: false,
}); // Get the value of a property using the Reflect API
const age = Reflect.get(obj, 'age');
console.log(age); // Invoke a method using the Reflect API
Reflect.apply(console.log, console, ['Hello, world!']);

This example shows how to use the Reflect API to define object properties, access object properties, and invoke object methods in a more flexible and consistent way.

4. Symbols

Symbols are a feature of JavaScript that allow you to create unique, immutable values that can be used as object keys or method names. Symbols can be used to implement advanced features like object privacy, method customization, and interface design.

This can help you implement object privacy, since symbols cannot be accessed or modified by outside code. For example, you could use symbols to define private methods or properties that can only be accessed from within the object.

Use Case: A password manager application that needs to store user credentials securely could use symbols to define private methods and properties within the password object, preventing unauthorized access or modification.

Example Code:

// Define a symbol
const KEY = Symbol('my secret key'); // Create an object with a private property
const obj = { [KEY]: 'secret value', publicProperty: 'public value',
}; // Access the private property using the symbol
console.log(obj[KEY]);
console.log(obj.publicProperty);

This example shows how to use symbols to create unique, immutable values that can be used as object keys to implement object privacy.

5. Iterators and Generators

Iterators and Generators are powerful features of JavaScript that allow you to iterate over collections of values in a more flexible and efficient way. Iterators and Generators can be used to implement advanced features like lazy evaluation, infinite sequences, and custom data structures.

Use iterators and generators to control the iteration of objects and generate sequences of values. This can help you write more efficient and expressive code, as well as enable advanced features like lazy evaluation and infinite sequences. For example, you could use iterators and generators to implement lazy loading of data or generate infinite sequences of random numbers.

Use Case: A data visualization tool that needs to handle large or complex datasets could use iterators and generators to implement lazy loading of data, allowing only the necessary data to be loaded and displayed at any given time, improving performance and reducing memory usage.

Example Code:

// Define an iterable object
const obj = { data: [1, 2, 3, 4], [Symbol.iterator]() { let index = 0; return { next: () => { if (index < this.data.length) { return { value: this.data[index++], done: false }; } else { return { done: true }; } }, }; },
}; // Use the iterator to iterate over the object
for (let value of obj) { console.log(value);
} // Define a generator function
function* fibonacci() { let a = 0, b = 1; while (true) { yield b; [a, b] = [b, a + b]; }
} // Use the generator to generate an infinite sequence
const sequence = fibonacci();
console.log(sequence.next().value);
console.log(sequence.next().value);
console.log(sequence.next().value);

This example shows how to use iterators to implement custom iteration behavior for objects, and how to use generators to generate infinite sequences of values.

In Conclusion, having a solid grasp of these advanced JavaScript concepts can help you stand out in a crowded job market and impress your interviewers with your skills and knowledge. By understanding the benefits and use cases of web workers, proxies, the Reflect API, symbols, iterators, and generators, you can write more efficient, flexible, and maintainable code that meets the needs of your users and stakeholders. Whether you're a front-end developer, back-end developer, or full-stack developer, these concepts are essential to take your skills to the next level and achieve success in your career.

Items.Title: Angular vs React：你应该选择哪一个？ | Angular vs React: Which One Should You Choose?
Items.Description:
作为开发人员，为您的项目选择合适的框架可能是一项艰巨的任务。 有很多可用的选项，每个选项都有自己的优点和缺点。 当今最流行的两个前端框架是 Angular 和 React。 在这篇文章中，我们将比较每种方法的优势并帮助您做出明智的决定。

角度的优势

以下是使用 Angular 的一些优点：

稳健性和可扩展性

它的架构和内置工具使您可以随着项目的增长轻松扩展。

内置功能和工具

其中包括强大的 CLI、全面的路由功能和内置测试工具。

增强性能

它还支持服务器端渲染，可以进一步提高性能。

强依赖注入

这有助于使您的代码更易于维护和测试。

详细文档

这使您可以轻松找到问题的答案并在需要时获得帮助。

反应的优点

以下是使用 React 的一些优点：

轻巧灵活

这可以使其成为小型和简单项目的不错选择。

基于组件的架构

这有助于使您的代码更加模块化并且更易于维护。

易于学习和使用

其简单的语法和直观的 API 也使其易于使用，即使对于初学者也是如此。

庞大而活跃的社区

当您学习或解决问题时，这可能是宝贵的资源。

与其他库和框架无缝集成

那么，您应该选择哪一个？ 答案取决于您的具体需求和项目要求。 以下是一些需要考虑的因素：

  1. 项目的规模和复杂性：Angular 的健壮性和可扩展性使其成为大型复杂项目的不错选择，而 React 的灵活性和轻量级使其更适合小型项目。

  2. 学习曲线：React 具有更简单、更直观的 API，使其更易于学习和使用。 另一方面，Angular 的学习曲线更陡峭，但提供了更多的功能和工具。

  3. 与其他库和框架集成：React 的灵活性和易于集成使其成为需要互操作性的项目的不错选择。 社区支持：Angular 和 React 都有庞大而活跃的社区，因此寻找帮助和资源应该不是问题。 然而，React 更大的社区可能会更容易找到特定问题的帮助和资源。

总之，Angular 和 React 都有自己独特的优势，适合不同类型的项目。 如果您正在处理大型复杂的应用程序并且需要功能强大且功能丰富的框架，那么 Angular 可能是更好的选择。 但是，如果您正在处理一个更小或更简单的项目并且重视灵活性和易用性，那么 React 可能是您的不二之选。 最终，该决定应基于您的项目要求和个人喜好。 无论您选择什么，Angular 和 React 都有庞大而活跃的社区和大量文档，因此您将获得大量支持和资源供您使用。



原文:

As a developer, choosing the right framework for your project can be a daunting task. There are so many options available, each with their own advantages and disadvantages. Two of the most popular front-end frameworks today are Angular and React. In this post, we'll compare the advantages of each and help you make an informed decision.

Advantages of Angular

Angular is a powerful and robust framework that's known for its scalability and built-in features. Here are some of the advantages of using Angular:

Robustness and scalability

Angular is a full-featured framework that's designed to handle large and complex applications. Its architecture and built-in tools make it easy to scale up as your project grows.

Built-in features and tools

Angular comes with a wide range of built-in features and tools that make development faster and more efficient. These include a powerful CLI, comprehensive routing capabilities, and built-in testing tools.

Enhanced performance

Angular uses a powerful change detection mechanism that makes it one of the fastest front-end frameworks available. It also supports server-side rendering, which can further improve performance.

Strong dependency injection

Angular's dependency injection system makes it easy to manage dependencies and create modular code. This can help make your code more maintainable and easier to test.

Detailed documentation

Angular has extensive documentation and a large community of developers who contribute to its ongoing development. This makes it easy to find answers to your questions and get help when you need it.

Advantages of React

React is a lightweight and flexible framework that's known for its component-based architecture and ease of use. Here are some of the advantages of using React:

Lightweight and flexible

React is a library, not a full-featured framework, which means it's more lightweight and flexible than Angular. This can make it a good choice for small and simple projects.

Component-based architecture

React's component-based architecture makes it easy to create reusable components that can be used throughout your project. This can help make your code more modular and easier to maintain.

Easy to learn and use

React has a relatively shallow learning curve, which makes it easy to get started with. Its simple syntax and intuitive API also make it easy to use, even for beginners.

Large and active community

React has a large and active community of developers who contribute to its development and share knowledge and resources. This can be a valuable resource when you're learning or troubleshooting issues.

Seamless integration with other libraries and frameworks

React can be easily integrated with other libraries and frameworks, making it a good choice for projects that require flexibility and interoperability.

Comparison of Angular and React

So, which one should you choose? The answer depends on your specific needs and project requirements. Here are some factors to consider:

  1. Size and complexity of your project: Angular's robustness and scalability make it a good choice for large and complex projects, while React's flexibility and lightweight make it better suited for smaller projects.

  2. Learning curve: React has a simpler and more intuitive API, making it easier to learn and use. Angular, on the other hand, has a steeper learning curve but offers more features and tools.

  3. Integration with other libraries and frameworks: React's flexibility and ease of integration make it a good choice for projects that require interoperability. Angular has a strong ecosystem of built-in tools and features that make it less reliant on external libraries.

  4. Community support: Both Angular and React have large and active communities, so finding help and resources shouldn't be a problem. However, React's larger community may make it easier to find help and resources for specific issues.

In conclusion, both Angular and React have their own unique advantages and are well-suited for different types of projects. If you're working on a large and complex application and need a powerful and feature-rich framework, Angular may be the better choice. However, if you're working on a smaller or simpler project and value flexibility and ease of use, React may be the way to go. Ultimately, the decision should be based on your project requirements and personal preferences. Whatever you choose, both Angular and React have large and active communities and extensive documentation, so you'll have plenty of support and resources at your disposal.

Items.Title: 超越技术面试：重新思考顶尖人才的招聘标准。 | Beyond Technical Interviews: Rethinking Hiring Criteria for Top Talent.
Items.Description:
发表于 3 月 12 日

尽管技术面试是确定最合适候选人的常用工具，但它们往往会忽视许多优秀甚至是杰出员工的巨大潜力。

作为一名开发人员，我可以证明这一点。 我是一个始终不渝地解决问题的人，即使这些问题不在我指定的职责范围内。

我始终愿意将公司的重担扛在肩上，而且我的表现始终超出预期。 但是，在技术面试方面，我很挣扎。

无论是由于紧张还是其他因素，我可能无法像我知道的那样表现出色。

我的个人经历让我对如何为我未来的事业聘请最佳人选有了独特的见解。 我现在确信，技术面试不应该作为评估候选人是否合适的唯一标准。 在我的下一篇文章中，我将详细说明应该用于招聘的替代基准，以确保公司吸引和留住顶尖人才。

如果您觉得这篇文章有趣，请不要忘记鼓掌。

Atugonza ( Billions ) Joel

Posted on Mar 12

Although technical interviews are a commonly-used tool to identify the most suitable candidates, they often overlook the immense potential of many good or even exceptional employees.

As a developer myself, I can attest to this fact. I am someone who consistently goes above and beyond to resolve issues, even if they are not within my designated responsibilities.

I am always willing to carry the weight of the company on my shoulders, and I consistently exceed performance expectations. However, I struggle when it comes to technical interviews.

Whether due to nerves or other factors, I may not perform as well as I know I am capable of.

My personal experience has given me a unique perspective on how to hire the best candidates for my future endeavors. I am now convinced that technical interviews should not serve as the sole criterion for assessing the suitability of a candidate. In my next piece, I will elaborate on alternative benchmarks that should be used for hiring, in order to ensure that companies attract and retain top talent.

Items.Title: 使用 Granted AWS 就像 SSO 工具一样简单 | Easy as SSO tooling with Granted AWS
Items.Description:
毫无疑问， AWS SSO （或者我应该说 IAM Identity Centre？）是对 AWS 整体访问管理和安全性的重要补充。 但是，随着您在云中逐渐成熟，接触到AWS Organizations和架构良好的框架，您很快就会拥有许多 AWS 账户，并且管理所有这些账户有点糟糕。

我应该提一下，我真的不想再次更改工具，我使用了一堆不同的工具来管理 CLI 和 Web 访问，配置一个或多个新工具的想法听起来并不有趣，但我是也非常超过 AWS 的默认工具。

有什么问题？

好的，让我们使用上面的示例设置场景，您在同一个组织中有多个 AWS 帐户。 假设您有开发、非生产和生产账户，每个账户都是一个单独的 AWS 账户。

网络控制台

使用浏览器（选择你的毒药）跳转到你的 AWS SSO 启动 url 并选择帐户和角色以访问管理控制台。 这应该使用该帐户的管理控制台启动一个新的选项卡或窗口，现在为另一个帐户再次执行此操作......以前的会话不再有效。 假设您想比较管理中的开发和生产（很常见），您不能轻易做到（隐身/私人）。

本地 CLI

这个问题不大，但您可以使用aws cli使用您的组织 SSO 启动会话就好了。 当您有很多帐户时，它会很快失去控制，您必须检查您的配置或只记住您的配置文件的名称。

以前的解决方法

在 SSO 之前，有一些出色的项目确实起到了帮助作用：

这是一个巧妙的工具，可以用不同的颜色和名称标记您的浏览器会话，这使得您在哪个帐户中一目了然。我真的很喜欢扩展的这一部分以及能够轻松切换帐户。 前段时间它不支持 AWS SSO，但现在提到了它。

一个方便的小工具，用于将您的 SSO 会话令牌与旧式 AWS CLI 令牌同步，使其与其他工具（如 AWS CDK）一起使用。

你开始明白了，这只是几个例子，我没有提到任何想到的保险库或天蓝色登录工具。 账户访问和管理的状态分布在许多不同的项目中，是一个微妙的生态系统。

如果我要总结一下解决方案的外观：

  • 在基于 Web 的管理控制台中处理多个并发会话
  • 轻松识别您使用的帐户和角色
  • 无需记住所有详细信息即可管理多个帐户的 CLI 访问的更好方法
  • 理想情况下是一种管理批次的工具，无需同步等解决方法

搜索

与大多数问题一样，我从典型的 AWS SSO CLI 和控制台相关工具搜索开始，以寻求帮助。 对我来说最突出的一篇文章来自Corey Quinn -认为 aws 登录是理所当然的（有趣的事实：这篇文章的标题几乎完全相同，我什至没有注意到，你赢了这一轮 Corey）。 这篇文章真正击中了我遇到的问题，并建议使用Granted （github 链接）（ Granted.dev有一些不错的信息）。

现在对我来说，Granted 确实解决了 AWS CLI 和 Web 管理控制台中的所有缺点，尤其是关于多个帐户的缺点，而且还添加了一些我直到现在才知道我需要的不错的功能......

授予 AWS 登录

当我想到 Granted 时，它可以做我想做的一切，甚至更多，它超级容易安装、超级容易配置并且超级容易使用。 我不打算详细介绍安装和配置， Granted 文档非常好，而且比我说得更好。

我假设您已经配置了 aws CLI，我不会真正谈论创建配置的细节。

在 MacOS 上安装是一个简单的 brew tap and install，这将添加开发人员 repo 并安装 Granted 工具。 安装后，您需要运行配置。

开始之前 我强烈建议为您的浏览器安装 Firefox，因为容器比 Chrome 的隐身模式更好。 你自己帮个忙，安装 Firefox。

安装 Granted 后，配置非常简单，您会注意到一个程序granted可以从终端运行。 当您第一次运行它时，CLI 向导将指导您完成使用现有 aws 配置自动配置授权设置并将扩展添加到浏览器的步骤。

这通常需要不到一分钟的时间。

配置完成....

要使用granted您将从终端使用assume命令， assume命令将提供您想要假设的配置文件列表。 如果您还没有有效的令牌，它将运行您完成登录设置（类似于 AWS SSO），如果您有一个有效的会话，它将刷新您的令牌。

这是授权文档的屏幕截图，显示了运行 assume 时发生的情况，请注意类似菜单的下拉菜单，这是按频率排序的（可配置）：

如果您想启动 AWS Web 管理控制台会话，您只需添加一个参数assume -c 。 以下 granted 文档的屏幕截图显示了 Granted 在浏览器中的样子：

注意选项卡中不同颜色的线条，一条是蓝色的，一条是橙色的，请注意带有图标的橙色role-b 。 帮助您确定您所在的会话，但也允许并发会话，每个会话都在它自己的 firefox 容器中运行。

你想要更多？

就像我说的，Granted 做的更多，而且大多数选项在某种程度上都是可配置的：

而这样的例子不胜枚举...

最后的想法

停止你正在做的事情并设置当然，不是明天，不是下周，现在就做。 我保证这听起来很简单，您会希望早点完成它。 一切正常，它极大地改善了您的工作流程，并且更容易与其他人一起使用它。 如果您想了解今天讨论内容的具体信息，请务必查看 Granted 文档。 向 Last Week In AWS 的 Corey 大声喊叫，感谢他最初的文章让我朝着正确的方向前进。

您有适合您的替代方案吗？ 也许比 Granted 更好？ 请分享，我很想听听其他人关于什么对他们有用。

致谢： Studio Blackthorns在Unsplash上的文章照片



原文:

No doubt about it, AWS SSO (or should I say IAM Identity Centre?) is a great addition to the overall access management and security in AWS. But, as you mature in the cloud with a touch of AWS Organizations and dash of well-architected framework you'll soon have many AWS accounts and managing all of those accounts kind of sucks.

I should mention, I really didn't want to change tooling again, I was using a bunch of different tools to manage CLI and web access and the thought of configuring a new tool or tools didn't sound like a fun time but I was also very much over the default tools from AWS.

What's the problem?

OK, let's set the scene using the example above, you have multiple AWS accounts in the same organization. Let's say you have development, non-production and production accounts, each one being a separate AWS account.

Web Console

Using a browser (choose your poison) jump over to your AWS SSO start url and select the account and role to access the management console. This should launch a new tab or window with the management console for that account, now do it again for another account...the previous sessions is no longer valid. Let's say you wanted to compare development to production in the management (pretty common), you can't easily do that (incognito / private).

Local CLI

This one is less of issue but you can use the aws cli to start sessions using your organization SSO just fine. It can just get out of control quickly when you have many accounts, you have to review your config or just remember the names of your profiles.

Previous workarounds

Before SSO there were (are) a few brilliant projects out there that really helped out:

AWS Extend Switch Roles Chrome browser extension

This is a neat tool that would label your browser session with a different colour and name which made it obvious which account you were in. I really enjoyed this part of the extension as well as being able to switch accounts easily. It didn't support AWS SSO sometime ago but there is mention of it now.

YAWSSO cli tool

A handy little tool to sync up your SSO session tokens with the old style AWS CLI tokens to make it work with other tools like AWS CDK.

You start to get the idea, this is just a couple of examples, I didn't mention any vault or azure login tools which come to mind. The state of account access and management is spread across many different projects and is a delicate ecosystem.

If I were to summarize what a solution might look it:

  • Handle multiple concurrent sessions in the web based management console
  • Easily identify which account and role you're using
  • A better way to manage CLI access for multiple accounts without having to remember all of the details
  • Ideally one tool to manage the lot without workarounds for syncing etc

The search

Like most problems I started with the typical search for AWS SSO CLI and console related tools to help out. One of the write-ups that stood out for me was from Corey Quinn - taking aws logins for granted (fun fact: the title of this article was almost identical without me even noticing, you win this round Corey). The article really hit home the problems I was having and suggested the use of Granted (github link) (Granted.dev has some nice info).

Now for me, Granted really solved all of the shortcomings in the AWS CLI and web management console particularly around multiple accounts but also added a few nice features I didn't know I needed until now...

Granted AWS Logins

When I think about Granted it does everything that I wanted and more, it's super easy to install, super easy to configure and super easy to use. I'm not going to go into much detail on the install and configuration, the Granted docs are excellent and say it better than I can.

I'm going to assume you already have the aws CLI configured, I won't really be talking about creating configs in details.

Installation on MacOS is a simple brew tap and install, this will add the developer repo and install the Granted tools. Once it's installed you'll need to run through the configuration.

Before you begin I strongly recommend installing Firefox for your browser as containers are better than Chrome's incognito. Do you self a favour and install Firefox.

Once you've installed Granted the configuration is pretty simple, you'll notice a program granted which can be run from the terminal. When you run this the first time a CLI wizard will guide you through the steps to auto-configure your granted setup using your existing aws configs and add the extension to your browser.

This typically takes less than a minute.

Configuration done....

To use granted you will use the assume command from the terminal, the assumecommand will provide a list of profiles that you would like to assume. If you don't have a valid token already it will run you through the login setups (similar to AWS SSO), if you do have a valid session it will refresh your token.

Here is a screen shot from the granted docs showing what happens when you run assume, notice the dropdown like menu, this is ordered by frequency (configurable):

If you want to start an AWS web management console session you just add a parameter assume -c. The screenshot from the granted docs below showing what Granted looks like in the browser:

Notice the different colour lines in the tabs, one is blue and one is orange, notice the role-b in orange with an icon. Helping you to identify which session you are in but also allow concurrent sessions, each running in it's own firefox container.

You wanted more?

Like I said, Granted does much more and most of the options are configurable to some extent:

And the list goes on...

Final thoughts

Stop what you're doing and setup Granted, not tomorrow, not next week, just do it now. I promise it is a simple as it sounds and you'll be wishing you had done it sooner. Everything just works and it greatly improves your workflow and easier to work with others using it also. Be sure to check to the Granted docs if you want specifics on what was discussed today. Shout out to Corey from Last Week In AWS for the initial write up which got me headed in the right direction.

Do you have an alternative that works for you? Maybe better than Granted? Please share, I'd love to hear from others about what works for them.
